communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Nonprofit contracts and grant agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All contracts, grant agreements, amendments and payments between the City of Detroit and the following entities:

All Things Women
Cass Community Social Services
Coalition on Temporary Shelter (COTS)
Covenant House
Detroit Rescue Mission Ministries
Michigan Veterans Foundation
Neighborhood Service Organization (NSO)
Salvation Army Booth Services Center
St. Johns Community Center

This request includes:

-  Executed contracts and grant agreements, including amendments
-  Records sufficient to show the amount awarded, contract term and purpose of each agreement
-  Vendor payment records, including invoices, payment logs or disbursement records
-  Any available contract or vendor summary reports maintained by the City

Timeframe: Jan. 1, 2023 through April 8, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Mondry

communications.subject: Fw: [EXTERNAL] Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Nonprofit contracts and grant agreements
communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on April 8, 2026. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, April 9, 2026.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before April 30, 2026.

Your request is being handled by Ivars Steins. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Ivars Steins at Steinsi@detroitmi.gov<mailto:Steinsi@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

title: 8a Pass through entity scheme. (Small Business Administration)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: 8a Pass through entity scheme. (Small Business Administration)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide the status of the complaints filed against the small business Infer Solutions  and Globaltekforce for participating in the pass through entity schemes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RG

Your request sought the following information:

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, RG Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2261074/

Thank you

communications.communication:

Thank you.


[cid:image001.png@01DCC823.F4D71860]
Margaret Wang
Assistant Counsel to the Inspector General
Office of Inspector General
U.S. Small Business Administration

communications.communication:
